ai_strategy_lead.name: Sudeep Sarkar
ai_strategy_lead.title: Launch (Interim) Dean, Bellini College of Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity and Computing; Co-Director, Institute for Artificial Intelligence + X

documents.title: Bellini College of Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity and Computing launch
Generative AI at USF (genai.usf.edu) - central AI hub
USF plans to launch college focused on AI, cybersecurity and computing

ai_strategy.summary: USF's AI strategy centers on two complementary structures: the Bellini College of Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity and Computing, launched August 25, 2025 with a historic $40 million gift from Arnie and Lauren Bellini (the first U.S. college named for the convergence of AI and cybersecurity), and the university-wide Institute for Artificial Intelligence + X (AI+X) founded in 2019 to advance transdisciplinary AI research across healthcare, finance, biology, manufacturing, and transportation. These anchor a workforce-oriented agenda voiced by President Moez Limayem that positions USF as Tampa Bay's AI and cybersecurity engine ('Cyber Bay') and embeds AI into teaching, research, and administration. A centralized genAI portal (genai.usf.edu) coordinates guidance, tools (Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini), training, and task-force governance.

key_provisions: Human-centered approach: AI augments rather than replaces human intelligence, judgment, and critical thinking
Individual responsibility for validity, correctness, and usefulness of AI-generated content
Transparency: users must disclose AI-generated content in published works, official communications, and student submissions where permitted
Data protection: prohibits sharing student, employment, or protected information with public AI tools; must comply with FERPA and HIPAA
Prohibits uploading copyrighted/course materials (articles, books, lecture notes, images, videos, software, code) to publicly accessible chatbots for training
Microsoft Copilot Chat (via USF login) is the sanctioned, data-protected enterprise AI tool
Course-level use is at instructor discretion; syllabus guidance templates provided by the Provost
ai_policy.summary: USF operates under comprehensive university-level generative AI guidance coordinated through genai.usf.edu and the Office of the Provost. The guidance is permissive-with-guardrails: it encourages AI adoption across teaching, research and operations while enforcing FERPA/HIPAA compliance, data protection, transparency, and copyright respect. Classroom policy is delegated to instructors, supported by a Provost-provided syllabus template. Microsoft Copilot Chat is the sanctioned data-protected enterprise alternative to public tools like ChatGPT.

bodies.name: USF GenAI Task Force
bodies.scope: Interdisciplinary body appointed by the Provost and VP of IT to build AI governance, recommend best practices, anticipate workforce needs, and communicate AI initiatives to USF stakeholders

bodies.name: Bellini College of Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity and Computing
bodies.scope: University college unifying AI, cybersecurity and computing; houses academic programs, research centers, and industry engagement with an Academic Advisory Board and Corporate Advisory Board

bodies.name: Institute for Artificial Intelligence + X (AI+X)
bodies.scope: University-wide R&D institute coordinating externally-funded AI research across engineering, medicine, arts and sciences, business, and public health

bodies.name: Bellini College Academic Advisory Board
bodies.scope: Industry and academic advisors guiding program content and accreditation; members include CIO Sidney Fernandes, Microsoft's Bradley Lawrence, ReliaQuest's Kim Hill, and others

bodies.name: AI College Task Force (2024)
bodies.scope: Faculty-inclusive body convened to steer the academic plan for the new AI and cybersecurity college prior to its 2025 launch

governance_structure.summary: USF's AI governance is anchored by the GenAI Task Force (policy/guidance), the Bellini College (academic programs and research), and the AI+X Institute (university-wide research). A Bellini College Academic Advisory Board adds industry voice from Microsoft, ReliaQuest, PGIM, and USF IT leadership. The structure is multi-nodal rather than a single Chief AI Officer role.

initiatives.title: Bellini College of Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity and Computing
initiatives.description: USF launched the Bellini College on August 25, 2025 with a $40 million founding gift from Arnie and Lauren Bellini - the largest gift in USF's 70-year history and the first U.S. college named for the convergence of AI and cybersecurity. First-semester enrollment exceeded 3,000 students across undergraduate and graduate programs, with plans to reach 5,000 students and 100 faculty by 2028.
initiatives.significance: Establishes a named-college organizational form for AI + cybersecurity + computing - a structural bet few R1 peers have made, and a signal of state-level prioritization of AI workforce pipelines.

investment.funding_source: Arnie and Lauren Bellini (Bellini Capital); matching commitment up to $5M to raise additional $10M
investment.description: $40M naming gift plus a $5M 1:1 match to raise an additional $10M for the college

collaborations.description: Industry advisory board and embedded cyber labs (ReliaQuest Labs, Rapid7 Cyber Threat Intelligence Lab) with Tampa-area cyber firms

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a durable academic unit that will set programmatic, research and hiring benchmarks for AI education across Florida's SUS.

initiatives.title: Institute for Artificial Intelligence + X (AI+X)
initiatives.description: USF's university-wide R&D institute founded in July 2019 with a $120,000 Strategic Investment Pool allocation. By 2020 it grew to over 110 affiliated faculty from engineering, arts and sciences, medicine, and business, and attracted more than $1.7M in external research awards from NSF, DoD, and NIH. It hosts the annual AI+X Symposium and co-directs are Sudeep Sarkar and Lawrence Hall.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a transdisciplinary research-center model that pre-dates and now complements the Bellini College, with substantial federal grant capture.

investment.funding_source: NSF, DoD, NIH, DoE, state, industry; initial $120K from USF Strategic Investment Pool
investment.description: Founding seed of $120K USF internal; >$1.7M in sponsored research by 2020; CSE department executing $12M in active external AI-related grants

collaborations.description: Federal agency funding and state-level research alignment

initiatives.policy_implications: Anchors USF's externally-funded AI research portfolio; coordinates with Bellini College for talent and infrastructure.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365 campus rollout
initiatives.description: USF is a Microsoft reference customer for Copilot in higher education, beginning with a 150-license pilot call in early 2024 that received 500 requests within three days. Microsoft Copilot Chat is now available to all USF students and employees via USF login in a secure, encrypted environment where tenant data is not used to train models. Use cases span document drafting, meeting summarization, Help Desk ticket triage (via Azure OpenAI Service), and research assistance.
initiatives.significance: One of the higher-ed sector's most-cited Microsoft Copilot deployments, with measurable productivity and student-service impact.

investment.description: Licensing and integration costs not disclosed publicly; phased expansion from 150-seat pilot toward broader employee deployment

collaborations.description: Reference customer relationship with Microsoft; co-hosted Copilot webinars for USF faculty and staff

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Enterprise AI productivity suite across Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneDrive

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Data-protected chat interface for students and employees

tools_and_vendors.name: Azure OpenAI Service
tools_and_vendors.role: Powers USF Help Desk automation and chatbot classification

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets the enterprise default for sanctioned AI use and defines data-protection posture for FERPA/HIPAA-sensitive workflows.

initiatives.title: Bachelor of Science in Artificial Intelligence (BSAI) and MS in AI
initiatives.description: USF offers an undergraduate BSAI and a graduate MSAI through the Bellini College, covering machine learning, NLP, computer vision, robotics, and AI ethics. A Graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence and an 'AI and Everyday Impact: Applied Practices' graduate certificate round out the portfolio. A Computer Science + Business interdisciplinary track plus CS + Criminology and CS + Interdisciplinary Social Sciences provide AI in applied contexts, with CS + Economics and CS + Anthropology launching Fall 2027.
initiatives.significance: Full-stack AI degree portfolio from microcredential to PhD, combining pure AI and cross-disciplinary tracks - differentiating USF from peers who offer only certificates or concentrations.

investment.funding_source: Bellini College and State University System of Florida
investment.description: Program development and faculty hiring within Bellini College; 14 new faculty joined Fall 2025

partners: Center for Career and Professional Development
collaborations.description: Co-op and early internship pipeline beginning sophomore year

initiatives.policy_implications: Expands Florida's AI talent pipeline; explicit coverage of AI ethics curriculum ties to Responsible AI positioning.

initiatives.title: MS in Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics (Muma College of Business)
initiatives.description: USF's Muma College of Business offers an on-campus STEM-designated MS in AI and Business Analytics plus a Global Executive online variant. Curriculum blends seven data-focused courses with a 6-credit project; graduates are placed at Microsoft, IBM, Deloitte, Apple, JPMorgan Chase, and others. The program exemplifies AI-in-disciplines deployment outside the Bellini College.
initiatives.significance: Extends AI beyond a dedicated AI college into the business school, a pattern of 'AI everywhere' that competing R1s are adopting for their professional schools.

investment.description: Tuition-funded master's program operated by the business school

collaborations.description: Employer pipelines for STEM OPT-eligible graduates

initiatives.policy_implications: Diffuses AI skills into business curricula; complements the technical BSAI and MSAI from Bellini.

initiatives.title: Course Enhancement with Generative AI microcredential & faculty FLC
initiatives.description: USF Innovative Education runs a free, self-paced 'Course Enhancement with Generative AI' microcredential that launched March 15, 2024, an 'AI Teaching Transformations' customized department-level training program, and a monthly Faculty Learning Community (InEd AI FLC) held the last Friday of each month. Additional workshops, 'GenAI in Action' and 'AI Whisperer' microcredentials round out the faculty development portfolio.
initiatives.significance: Structured, recurring faculty development is a differentiator; most peer R1s run ad-hoc workshops rather than a named microcredential pathway.

investment.description: Staffing and platform costs not publicly disclosed; microcredentials are free to USF instructors

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Primary sanctioned AI tool covered in training

tools_and_vendors.name: Google Gemini
tools_and_vendors.role: Alternative data-protected AI tool available via USF Google Apps

initiatives.policy_implications: Operationalizes the university's permissive-with-guardrails posture through mass faculty upskilling.

initiatives.title: USF GenAI Task Force and genai.usf.edu central hub
initiatives.description: A provost-and-CIO-appointed interdisciplinary GenAI Task Force provides university-wide AI governance, drafts AI guidance, anticipates workforce needs, and communicates AI initiatives. The task force operates the genai.usf.edu hub, which centralizes policy, approved tools (Copilot, Gemini), training resources, events, and a story submission channel.
initiatives.significance: Centralized AI governance and communications hub - a pattern several Florida SUS peers are beginning to replicate as the state Task Force on AI and Cybersecurity issues recommendations.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Provost and VP Information Technology

partners: Florida Board of Governors Task Force on AI and Cybersecurity
collaborations.description: USF's approach aligns with state-level SUS task force deliberations chaired by Edward Haddock

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a durable, visible governance surface that can be evolved as state or federal AI policy matures.

initiatives.title: USF Health Aimee AI voice agent and clinical AI deployments
initiatives.description: USF Health's Morsani College of Medicine operates 'Aimee,' an AI voice agent that has handled over 600,000 patient calls, reducing call abandonment by 56% and increasing scheduled visits by 21% - productivity equivalent to 40 additional staff. USF Health additionally uses Nuance DAX Ambient AI for clinical note drafting and Epic's AI tools for message triage and chart summarization.
initiatives.significance: A concrete enterprise-scale AI deployment in an academic medical center with measurable operational impact; exemplifies AI in healthcare administration distinct from research AI.

investment.description: Licensing and integration costs not disclosed; estimated savings equivalent to ~40 FTEs

tools_and_vendors.name: Aimee
tools_and_vendors.role: AI voice agent for patient call handling

initiatives.policy_implications: HIPAA-compliant AI use in a large academic medical enterprise; sets operational precedents for clinical-AI governance.

initiatives.title: Tampa Bay Cyber Labs: ReliaQuest Labs and Rapid7 Cyber Threat Intelligence Lab
initiatives.description: Bellini College students gain hands-on industry training through the ReliaQuest Labs program (more than 300 USF students hired by ReliaQuest since 2017-18) and the Rapid7 Cyber Threat Intelligence Lab on campus. The partnerships combine curriculum, internships, and simulation-based research in AI-adjacent cybersecurity.
initiatives.significance: Deep industry-academic workforce pipelines are a signature differentiator for USF and a model for regional cyber-tech clusters.

investment.description: Sponsored lab facilities and cohort-based internships

collaborations.description: Embedded co-op labs with Tampa cybersecurity employers

initiatives.policy_implications: Reinforces Tampa as 'Cyber Bay' and demonstrates how AI-adjacent talent development can scale through company-hosted labs.

initiatives.title: NSF Southeast I-Corps Hub participation
initiatives.description: The Bellini College is a partner in the NSF Southeast I-Corps Hub, a multi-institution effort to accelerate university research into commercial products. USF's share of the award is approximately $1.6 million supporting innovation and entrepreneurship tied to AI, cybersecurity and computing research.
initiatives.significance: Connects USF's AI research output to a federal translation-and-commercialization pipeline - a vector for startup-academic and industry impact.

investment.description: Federal award for I-Corps regional innovation hub activity

initiatives.policy_implications: Supports translation of AI research into ventures aligned with regional cyber-tech cluster.

initiatives.title: Bellini Center for Talent Development in Muma College of Business
initiatives.description: The Bellinis' prior $11 million gift established the Bellini Center for Talent Development in USF's Muma College of Business, which supports career readiness and AI-adjacent skills programming. It preceded and complements the larger 2025 AI/cyber college gift.
initiatives.significance: A philanthropic through-line from career-readiness to AI-specific investment signals sustained donor strategy, not a one-off gift.

investment.description: $11M naming gift to the Muma College of Business

collaborations.description: Talent-pipeline partnerships with Tampa Bay employers

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes donor precedent for USF's broader AI investment strategy.

initiatives.title: USF arts and sciences AI-era literacy faculty hire and curriculum reframing
initiatives.description: The USF College of Arts and Sciences hired an assistant professor dedicated to reframing literacy for the age of generative AI, supporting student writers navigating ChatGPT, Copilot, and Gemini. The hire signals AI's diffusion into humanities instruction and equity-of-access concerns around AI tool literacy.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates that AI strategy at USF extends beyond CS/engineering into the humanities - a structural answer to 'liberal arts in the AI era'.

investment.funding_source: USF College of Arts and Sciences

initiatives.policy_implications: Models how a humanities college can integrate AI without surrendering disciplinary identity.

initiatives.title: Innovative Education GenAI course-redesign support
initiatives.description: USF Innovative Education publishes GenAI teaching resources, faculty use cases, course-design support, and a microcredential that includes assessment, content, and feedback planning.
initiatives.significance: USF documents practical assessment and course redesign support rather than only high-level AI principles.

regulatory_context.summary: Florida's State University System Board of Governors established a Task Force on Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity chaired by Edward Haddock, which is expected to issue recommendations on AI use across the 12 public universities. The task force is active but has not produced a uniform system-wide AI policy; individual SUS institutions (including USF) set their own guidance. At the state level, Governor Ron DeSantis proposed a Citizen Bill of Rights for Artificial Intelligence, and a K-12 AI task force operates under fl-aitaskforce.org. USF's work is aligned with but not dictated by these state efforts.

narrative_summary: The University of South Florida has built one of the most structurally ambitious AI postures in U.S. higher education. Its centerpiece is the Bellini College of Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity and Computing - launched August 25, 2025 with a $40 million gift from Arnie and Lauren Bellini, the first named college in the nation dedicated to the convergence of AI and cybersecurity. First-semester enrollment exceeded 3,000 students, with a stated goal of 5,000 students and 100 faculty by 2028. Fourteen new faculty joined the college at launch under Interim Dean Sudeep Sarkar. The Bellini College rests on two decades of AI research leadership rooted in Kevin Bowyer's pioneering computer-vision work (1984-era) and sustained by Lawrence Hall and Sarkar, and it is complemented by the university-wide Institute for Artificial Intelligence + X (AI+X), founded in 2019 and now coordinating faculty across engineering, medicine, business, and the social sciences. Enterprise AI is led by CIO Sidney Fernandes, whose team partnered with Microsoft on a widely-cited Copilot for Microsoft 365 rollout (150-license pilot saturated within three days by 500 requests), campus-wide Copilot Chat and Gemini, and an Azure OpenAI Help Desk bot. USF Health's Aimee voice agent has handled 600,000 patient calls with measurable productivity wins. Governance is multi-nodal: a Provost-and-CIO-appointed GenAI Task Force runs the genai.usf.edu hub, publishes FERPA/HIPAA-aware guidance, and coordinates with the Florida Board of Governors' Task Force on AI and Cybersecurity. Faculty development runs through a Course Enhancement with Generative AI microcredential, AI Teaching Transformations, and a monthly InEd AI Faculty Learning Community. Weaknesses include the absence of a named Chief AI Officer (authority is distributed between the provost, CIO, and Bellini dean), no public flagship GPU supercomputer comparable to UF's HiPerGator, and only emerging literature on AI equity and digital divide. Under new president Moez Limayem (confirmed December 2025, began February 2026), the trajectory is toward 'Cyber Bay' workforce prominence, deeper CENTCOM-adjacent defense ties, and continued scaling of the Bellini College.
